updates.summary: This is a major version upgrade from TypeScript 4.8.3 to 6.0.2, skipping the entire 5.x release line. Major TypeScript releases typically introduce breaking changes that can affect compilation, type checking, and require code modifications. The changelog provides minimal detail, only referencing external resources for specific changes.
breakingChanges: Potential breaking changes in type system and compiler behavior between major versions
Possible deprecation or removal of legacy TypeScript features
Changes to module resolution and compilation target defaults
Updates to strict type checking rules and inference
ramifications: Existing TypeScript code may fail to compile without modifications
Build systems and tooling configurations may need updates
Third-party type definitions may be incompatible with the new version
Development team will need time to review and adapt to new TypeScript features and breaking changes
Extensive testing required across the entire codebase to ensure compatibility
